rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE expressions
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by respecting AADL scope rules
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its ports in AGREE expressions
Preserve existing AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
